operating_state: Netflix generated $8.16 billion in Q1 2023 revenue, a 4% increase driven by 4% growth in average paying memberships to 231.6 million, partially offset by 1% decline in average monthly revenue per paying membership to $11.70. Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenue by approximately $346 million, with international markets comprising 57% of total revenue and positive net membership additions of 1.8 million globally. Content amortization increased 9% while content cash payments decreased 28%, reflecting timing differences in production cycles.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 12%, representing 59% of revenues compared to 54% in the prior year, driven by higher content amortization. Operating margin compressed to 21% from 25% as operating expenses grew faster than revenue due to foreign exchange headwinds and higher personnel costs supporting streaming improvements and international expansion. The effective tax rate decreased to 11% from 19% primarily due to reduced foreign taxes and international provisions of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.
material_events: Reed Hastings transitioned from co-CEO to Executive Chairman effective January 13, 2023, with Greg Peters appointed as co-CEO alongside Ted Sarandos. The company repurchased $400 million of common stock during the quarter under its $5 billion authorization, with $4.0 billion remaining available. Netflix amended its revolving credit facility in March 2023 to replace LIBOR with Term SOFR rate and announced plans to discontinue its DVD-by-mail service in April 2023.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure intensified with 57% of revenue denominated in non-USD currencies, primarily euro, British pound, Brazilian real, Canadian dollar, Mexican peso, Japanese yen, and Australian dollar. The company recognized a $107 million foreign exchange loss primarily from euro-denominated debt remeasurement, compared to a $162 million gain in the prior year period. Netflix does not hedge foreign currency exposures, increasing vulnerability to exchange rate volatility as international expansion continues.
forward_outlook: Management maintains its strategy to grow the business globally while investing significantly in content, with total content obligations of $21.5 billion. The company expects more limited future capital needs from debt markets compared to prior years, with free cash flow generation capability supporting continued content investment and capital allocation flexibility.
uncertainty_profile: Content obligations include $14.3 billion not reflected on balance sheets that did not meet recognition criteria, with unknown future title commitments potentially ranging $1-4 billion over three years based on management judgment. The company maintains ongoing IRS examinations and debt maturities with euro-denominated notes subject to remeasurement volatility.
